_doc: Cross-platform golden samples for the `subscription-userinfo` HTTP header parser (audit C6 / D3c-03). This single file is the language-agnostic contract that every implementation of the parser must satisfy: the JS reference (src/utils/profile-info.ts), the native Kotlin `parseUserinfo` (android .../helper/BackgroundConfigWorker.kt) and Swift `parseUserinfo` (ios/core/BackgroundConfigRefresh.swift). Each language has a minimal runner that loads THIS file and asserts its parser matches. Do not inline-copy these cases into a runner; extend them here.

cases.name: order-independent and tolerant of extra whitespace/fields
a single present field, others default to 0
empty header string is all zeros
null / absent header is all zeros
left boundary is unanchored: `total=` matches inside `subtotal=` (shared quirk)
realistic multi-GB quota (100 GiB) is within the JS-safe integer range and parses identically on all platforms

knownDivergences.name: unlimited marker total=2^64-1 overflows Int64 on native but not JS
knownDivergences.note: Airports encode 'unlimited' as total=2^64-1. JS parseInt keeps a lossy float (> 1e19); Kotlin toLongOrNull / Swift Int64() overflow to 0. This split is NOT yet unified — it is a Batch 4 contract change (needs a shared 'unlimited' sentinel + device verification). Locked here so any accidental change to one side is caught.
